inferences.statement: From 'It was the case that Mary was not at home', can we infer 'It is not the case that it has always been the case that Mary was at home'?
From 'It was the case that the griffin was not in the forest', can we infer 'It is not the case that it has always been the case that the griffin was in the forest'?
From 'It was the case that the book was not at the table', can we infer 'It is not the case that it has always been the case that the book was in the table'?
From 'It was the case that it was not raining', can we infer 'It is not the case that it has always been the case that it was raining'?
From 'It was the case that Jeff was not playing basketball', can we infer 'It is not the case that it has always been the case that Jeff was playing basketball'?
From 'It was the case that Sue was not eating pizza', can we infer 'It is not the case that it has always been the case that Sue was eating pizza'?
From 'It was the case that Alex was not running', can we infer 'It is not the case that it has always been the case that Alex was running'?
From 'It was the case that the vampire was not awake', can we infer 'It is not the case that it has always been the case that the vampire was awake'?
From 'It was the case that Lisa was not wearing the red shirt', can we infer 'It is not the case that it has always been the case that Lisa was wearing the red shirt'?
From 'It was the case that the dog was not inside', can we infer 'It is not the case that it has always been the case that the dog was inside'?
From 'It was the case that the newspaper was not in the driveway', can we infer 'It is not the case that it has always been the case that the newspaper is in the driveway'?
From 'It was the case that the jacket was not draped over the chair', can we infer 'It is not the case that it has always been the case that the jacket is draped over the chair'?
From 'It was the case that the toy was not in the toy box', can we infer 'It is not the case that it has always been the case that the toy is in the toy box'?
From 'It was the case that the milk was not on the counter', can we infer 'It is not the case that it has always been the case that the milk is on the counter'?
From 'It was the case that the shoes were not in the closet', can we infer 'It is not the case that it has always been the case that the shoes are in the closet'?
From 'It was the case that the pen was not in my bag', can we infer 'It is not the case that it has always been the case that the pen is in my bag'?
From 'It was the case that the scarf was not stuffed in my backpack', can we infer 'It is not the case that it has always been the case that the scarf is stuffed in my backpack'?
From 'It was the case that the notebook was not in the drawer', can we infer 'It is not the case that it has always been the case that the notebook is in the drawer'?
From 'It was the case that the watch was not charging on the dresser', can we infer 'It is not the case that it has always been the case that the watch is charging on the dresser'?
From 'It was the case that the sunglasses were not in her bag', can we infer 'It is not the case that it has always been the case that the sunglasses are in her bag'?
